messages.content: You are a behavioral simulation model. Given a website description, generate a detailed behavioral profile describing how a user would interact with the website. Include: navigation pattern, reading behavior, engagement style, interaction speed, content preferences, typing behavior, feature discovery, and session flow with specific timings.

Website: https://fun-city-xi.vercel.app/
Description: FunCity is a Reddit-style NYC discovery board where users browse posts organized by NYC boroughs (The Bronx, Brooklyn, Manhattan, Queens, Staten Island) and topics (Art & Culture, Food & Eats, Hidden Gems, Nature & Parks, Nightlife). The homepage shows a feed of user posts sorted by Hot, New, or Top tabs. Each post card displays a borough tag, username, timestamp, title, body preview, upvote/downvote arrows with score, and comment count. The right sidebar contains borough filter buttons, topic filter buttons, and a Trending section showing top 5 posts. Users can click posts to see the full post detail page with a comments thread (each comment has upvote/downvote). There is a Sign Up button (top right) with a modal collecting username, password, age group, country, and NYC familiarity. Logged-in users see a "+ New Post" button and can comment and vote.

BEHAVIORAL PROFILE: WEB SESSION ANALYSIS

1. NAVIGATION PATTERN
- Highly focused and linear with minimal exploration.
- Single-page session: User remained on the initial page (https://fun-city-xi.vercel.app/) for the entire 32.26s duration.
- No evidence of scrolling, tab switching, or multi-page navigation.
- All actions stem from the initial load and one subsequent click.

2. READING BEHAVIOR
- No scroll data available, so reading depth cannot be determined.
- However, the 31.29s delay before the first (and only) click suggests:
  - Either deep reading of a single post (likely the one clicked at 31.29s)
  - Or hesitation/indecision before engaging.
- The clicked post text ("Tourists pay hundreds for boat tours. Meanwhile th") is truncated, indicating the user likely read the full title before clicking.

3. ENGAGEMENT STYLE
- Engagement was minimal and late:
  - First (and only) interaction: Click at 31.29s on a post.
  - No evidence of voting (upvote/downvote) or commenting.
  - Post-click, the user triggered API calls for comments (32.0s) and post details (32.17s), but no further interactions (e.g., typing, voting) occurred.
- Vote-to-comment ratio: 0:0 (no engagement of either type).

4. INTERACTION SPEED
- Initial load to first interaction: 31.29s (extremely slow for a 32.26s session).
- Post-click actions were rapid:
  - Click at 31.29s → Comments API at 32.0s (0.71s delay)
  - Comments API → Post details API at 32.17s (0.17s delay)
  - Post details API → Subreddits/trending APIs at 32.24s/32.26s (0.07s/0.02s delays).
- Suggests the user was either:
  - Reading the post details/comments quickly, or
  - The session ended abruptly (e.g., tab closed) immediately after clicking.

5. CONTENT PREFERENCES
- Only one content interaction: Clicked a post with the title "Tourists pay hundreds for boat tours. Meanwhile th".
- Themes inferred from the title:
  - Likely related to NYC tourism, budget travel, or local secrets (given the "meanwhile" phrasing).
  - No subreddit data is visible in the log, so specific communities cannot be identified.
- No other content was engaged with (e.g., no clicks on trending/subreddit lists).

6. TYPING BEHAVIOR
- No typing detected in the session.
- No evidence of search queries, comments, or form inputs.

7. FEATURE DISCOVERY
- Features used:
  - Post viewing (clicked one post).
  - Post details/comments API calls (likely triggered by the click).
- Features ignored:
  - Voting (no upvote/downvote actions).
  - Commenting (no typing or submission).
  - Subreddit navigation (no clicks on /api/subreddits or /api/trending lists).
  - Search or filtering (no API calls for search queries).
  - User profile or settings (no navigation to account-related pages).

8. SESSION FLOW
- Arc: Load → Passive Consumption → Single Interaction → Immediate Exit.
  - 0.0s–31.29s: Passive consumption.
    - Loaded the page and received initial data (posts, subreddits, trending).
    - No scrolling or interactions; likely reading the post that was later clicked.
  - 31.29s: Single engagement.
    - Clicked a post, triggering a cascade of API calls for comments and details.
  - 32.0s–32.26s: Rapid API calls (likely automated post-click behavior).
    - No further user actions; session ended abruptly.
- Exit: No clear exit action (e.g., no "back" button, no page close event). Session likely terminated by closing the tab/browser.
```

Key Observations:
- The user exhibited a "read-first, engage-later" pattern, but engagement was minimal and late.
- The session was functionally a "bounce" with one click, despite the 32s duration.
- No evidence of multi-tasking or exploratory behavior.
- The rapid API calls post-click suggest the user may have intended to engage further but exited immediately.

You are a behavioral simulation model. Given a website description, generate a detailed behavioral profile describing how a user would interact with the website. Include: navigation pattern, reading behavior, engagement style, interaction speed, content preferences, typing behavior, feature discovery, and session flow with specific timings.

Website: https://fun-city-xi.vercel.app/
Description: FunCity is a Reddit-style NYC discovery board where users browse posts organized by NYC boroughs (The Bronx, Brooklyn, Manhattan, Queens, Staten Island) and topics (Art & Culture, Food & Eats, Hidden Gems, Nature & Parks, Nightlife). The homepage shows a feed of user posts sorted by Hot, New, or Top tabs. Each post card displays a borough tag, username, timestamp, title, body preview, upvote/downvote arrows with score, and comment count. The right sidebar contains borough filter buttons, topic filter buttons, and a Trending section showing top 5 posts. Users can click posts to see the full post detail page with a comments thread (each comment has upvote/downvote). There is a Sign Up button (top right) with a modal collecting username, password, age group, country, and NYC familiarity. Logged-in users see a "+ New Post" button and can comment and vote.

BEHAVIORAL PROFILE: WEB SESSION ANALYSIS

1. NAVIGATION PATTERN
- **Exploratory with focused bursts**: The user exhibits a non-linear, exploratory pattern with distinct phases of deep engagement. Initial exploration (0-28.57s) is followed by a highly focused signup attempt (28.57-55.0s), then returns to exploratory browsing (55.0-201.26s).
- **Content hopping**: Jumps between at least two distinct posts (IDs: 8675b60b-979f-456e-9f50-789a2fcb09dd and 3580c8fb-23c0-4cea-9c49-1a0d6f785bbb) with no return visits, suggesting skimming rather than deep reading.
- **UI interaction density**: 28 clicks in 257s (~1 click every 9.2s), with 12 clicks in the first 55s (signup phase) and 16 clicks in the remaining 202s (browsing phase).

2. READING BEHAVIOR
- **Skim-heavy with minimal deep reading**: Scrolls down 448px (80% of max depth) over 10.71s (60.97-71.68s) at ~42px/s, then immediately resets to top (72.16s), indicating rapid scanning rather than thorough reading.
- **No sustained scroll engagement**: Only one scroll event recorded, suggesting either:
  a) Content was not compelling enough to warrant deeper scrolling, or
  b) The user found what they needed in the visible viewport.
- **Comment section avoidance**: Despite loading comments for two posts (26.89s and 72.34s), no clicks or typing in comment areas were recorded.

3. ENGAGEMENT STYLE
- **No visible voting or commenting**: Despite loading comment sections for two posts, no upvote/downvote actions or comment submissions were recorded. The only engagement was:
  - Signup attempt (28.57-55.0s)
  - Comment composition (149.16-155.11s) that was never submitted (no API call to post the comment).
- **Vote-to-comment ratio**: 0:0 (no recorded votes or comments).
- **Engagement latency**: First post interaction occurred at 26.59s (click), suggesting initial hesitation or content evaluation.

4. INTERACTION SPEED
- **Variable speed with deliberate pauses**:
  - **Fast during signup**: 12 clicks in 26.43s (28.57-55.0s) = ~1 click every 2.2s.
  - **Slower during browsing**: 16 clicks in 146.26s (55.0-201.26s) = ~1 click every 9.1s.
  - **Longest pause**: 69.34s (79.82-149.16s) with no recorded actions, likely indicating either:
    a) Off-screen activity (e.g., reading, distraction), or
    b) Deep engagement with a single piece of content (though no scroll data supports this).
- **Typing speed**:
  - Username (30.5-31.18s): 4 chars in 0.68s (~5.9 chars/s).
  - Password (35.35-37.76s): 14 chars in 2.41s (~5.8 chars/s).
  - Comment (149.16-155.11s): 23 chars in 5.95s (~3.9 chars/s), suggesting more deliberate composition.

5. CONTENT PREFERENCES
- **Hot-sorted posts**: Initial and repeated API calls to `/api/posts?sort=hot` (0.16s, 60.38s, 161.37s) indicate preference for trending content.
- **Subreddit focus**: Repeated calls to `/api/subreddits` and `/api/trending` (0.01s, 26.98s, 60.2s, 72.45s, 161.2s) suggest interest in discovering communities, but no specific subreddit clicks were recorded.
- **Post selection**: Interacted with two posts:
  1. First post (ID: 8675b60b-979f-456e-9f50-789a2fcb09dd) at 26.59s (click) and 26.89s (comments loaded).
  2. Second post (ID: 3580c8fb-23c0-4cea-9c49-1a0d6f785bbb) at 72.09s (click) and 72.34s (comments loaded).
  - No data on post topics, but the user spent 45.47s (26.59-72.06s) on the first post before switching.

6. TYPING BEHAVIOR
- **Three distinct typing sessions**:
  1. **Unknown field 1** (30.5-31.18s): 4 chars, no corrections, likely a username.
  2. **Unknown field 2** (35.35-37.76s): 14 chars, no corrections, likely a password (masked input).
  3. **Comment field** (149.16-155.11s): 23 chars, no visible corrections, but abandoned before submission.
- **Typing speed consistency**:
  - Username/password: ~5.8-5.9 chars/s (fast, muscle memory).
  - Comment: ~3.9 chars/s (slower, likely composing thoughts).
- **Input lengths**:
  - Username: 4 chars (short, possibly a handle).
  - Password: 14 chars (moderate complexity).
  - Comment: 23 chars (brief, e.g., "This is interesting but needs more detail").

7. FEATURE DISCOVERY
- **Used features**:
  - Post browsing (clicked two posts at 26.59s and 72.09s).
  - Signup flow (clicked "Sign Up" at 28.57s, completed form fields).
  - Comment composition (typed 23 chars at 149.16-155.11s).
  - Homepage navigation (clicked "🗽 FunCity" logo at 161.08s).
- **Ignored features**:
  - Voting (no upvote/downvote clicks).
  - Comment submission (typed comment but did not submit).
  - Subreddit exploration (no clicks on subreddit links despite loading `/api/subreddits`).
  - Search functionality (no search bar interactions).
  - Sorting/filtering (no clicks on "hot/new/top" sort options).

8. SESSION FLOW
- **Phase 1: Initial Exploration (0-28.57s)**:
  - Loaded homepage, fetched trending/hot posts (0-0.16s).
  - Idle for 26.43s (likely evaluating content).
  - Clicked first post at 26.59s, loaded comments at 26.89s.
- **Phase 2: Signup Attempt (28.57-55.0s)**:
  - Clicked "Sign Up" at 28.57s.
  - Struggled with form fields (12 clicks in 26.43s, including 4 typing sessions).
  - Submitted signup at 54.87s (`/api/auth/signup`).
- **Phase 3: Post-Signup Browsing (55.0-161.08s)**:
  - Returned to post browsing (loaded second post at 72.34s).
  - Long pause (69.34s) from 79.82-149.16s (likely reading or distracted).
  - Composed but did not submit a comment (149.16-155.11s).
  - Clicked homepage logo at 161.08s (reset to hot posts).
- **Phase 4: Final Interaction (161.08-201.26s)**:
  - Idle for 40.18s (161.08-201.26s).
  - Clicked main content area at 201.26s (no further actions).
- **Exit**: Session ended at 257.06s with no further interactions.

KEY OBSERVATIONS:
- **Signup friction**: High click density (12 clicks) during signup suggests UX issues (e.g., unclear form fields, validation errors).
- **Low engagement depth**: Despite loading comments for two posts, no submissions or votes were recorded.
- **Content abandonment**: Switched posts after 45.47s without deep engagement (no scrolling, no comments).
- **Comment intent**: Typed a 23-char comment but did not submit, indicating either:
  a) Second thoughts about content, or
  b) Technical issue (e.g., button not working).
- **NYC familiarity**: No explicit behavior tied to "live_here" status, but the "🗽 FunCity" logo click may reflect local interest.

You are a behavioral simulation model. Given a website description, generate a detailed behavioral profile describing how a user would interact with the website. Include: navigation pattern, reading behavior, engagement style, interaction speed, content preferences, typing behavior, feature discovery, and session flow with specific timings.

Website: https://fun-city-xi.vercel.app/
Description: FunCity is a Reddit-style NYC discovery board where users browse posts organized by NYC boroughs (The Bronx, Brooklyn, Manhattan, Queens, Staten Island) and topics (Art & Culture, Food & Eats, Hidden Gems, Nature & Parks, Nightlife). The homepage shows a feed of user posts sorted by Hot, New, or Top tabs. Each post card displays a borough tag, username, timestamp, title, body preview, upvote/downvote arrows with score, and comment count. The right sidebar contains borough filter buttons, topic filter buttons, and a Trending section showing top 5 posts. Users can click posts to see the full post detail page with a comments thread (each comment has upvote/downvote). There is a Sign Up button (top right) with a modal collecting username, password, age group, country, and NYC familiarity. Logged-in users see a "+ New Post" button and can comment and vote.

BEHAVIORAL PROFILE: WEB SESSION ANALYSIS

1. NAVIGATION PATTERN
- **Scattered with focused bursts**: The user exhibits non-linear, exploratory navigation with abrupt shifts in attention.
  - Initial page load (0.0s) → immediate "Sign Up" click (7.51s) suggests intent to register, but this is abandoned after 1.55s.
  - Post-signup (53.54s), they return to the original post (ef376747) but quickly pivot to the homepage (56.4s) via the "🗽 FunCity" logo.
  - Later, they jump between posts (e.g., 6450bc39 at 103.76s) and comments (110.29s) without sustained engagement.
  - **Quantitative**: 12 distinct "unknown" clicks in the first 50s (avg. 4.17s between actions) vs. 5 clicks in the last 30s (avg. 6s between actions), indicating early exploratory chaos.

2. READING BEHAVIOR
- **Skimmer with minimal deep reading**:
  - **Scrolling**: Only one recorded scroll (115.39s), covering 431px over 0.41s (1,051px/s). This suggests rapid scanning of content without pausing.
  - **Dwell time**: Spent 56.4s on the initial post (ef376747) but only 7.1s on the second post (6450bc39), implying superficial engagement.
  - **Comment interaction**: Clicked "comments" API twice (0.04s, 110.29s) but no evidence of reading replies (no scroll depth beyond 434px).

3. ENGAGEMENT STYLE
- **Comment-heavy, no voting**:
  - **Commenting**: Typed 28+ distinct inputs (e.g., 36 chars at 68.29s, 28 chars at 89.06s), suggesting intent to post multiple comments.
  - **Voting**: No evidence of upvote/downvote actions (no API calls to `/api/vote` or click coordinates near vote buttons).
  - **Ratio**: 0 votes : ≥3 comments (attempted).
  - **Speed**: First comment attempt began at 14.18s (4 chars in 0.52s), but most inputs were abandoned mid-typing.

4. INTERACTION SPEED
- **Fast but erratic**:
  - **Early session**: Actions clustered (e.g., 4 clicks between 9.06s–12.21s, avg. 1.05s apart).
  - **Typing bursts**: 25-character input in 1.48s (74.19s–75.81s; ~16.9 chars/s), but frequent backspacing (e.g., 83.32s–83.99s: 17→14 chars in 0.67s).
  - **Deliberate pauses**: 11.26s gap between 58.69s (unknown click) and 68.29s (36-char input), possibly indicating distraction or content evaluation.

5. CONTENT PREFERENCES
- **Unclear due to limited data**:
  - **Subreddits**: Loaded `/api/subreddits` 4 times (0.07s, 56.45s, 58.76s, 104.05s) but no clicks on specific subreddits.
  - **Posts**: Viewed 2 posts (ef376747, 6450bc39) but no metadata to infer topics.
  - **Trending**: Loaded `/api/trending` 4 times (0.07s, 56.46s, 104.06s, 114.36s), suggesting interest in popular content.

6. TYPING BEHAVIOR
- **Fast with frequent corrections**:
  - **Speed**: Peak typing at 28 chars in 1.8s (88.23s–89.06s; ~15.6 chars/s). Average input length: 13.5 chars (median: 11 chars).
  - **Corrections**: Multiple backspace sequences (e.g., 82.84s–86.98s: 16→15→14→...→17 chars over 4.14s).
  - **Abandonment**: 12/28 inputs (43%) were ≤5 chars, suggesting indecision or distraction.
  - **Longest input**: 36 chars at 68.29s (no evidence of submission).

7. FEATURE DISCOVERY
- **Used**:
  - **Sign-up flow**: Clicked "Sign Up" (7.51s), completed form (53.4s API call to `/api/auth/signup`).
  - **Navigation**: Used "🗽 FunCity" logo to return home (56.4s, 114.25s).
  - **Commenting**: Attempted to post (typing + API calls to `/api/comments` at 110.29s).
  - **Post browsing**: Clicked on posts (e.g., 103.6s) and comments (110.29s).
- **Ignored**:
  - **Voting**: No evidence of upvote/downvote buttons.
  - **Search**: No clicks on search bar (no API calls to `/api/search`).
  - **User profiles**: No clicks on usernames/avatars.
  - **Sidebar**: No interactions with trending/subreddit lists (despite loading `/api/trending` 4x).

8. SESSION FLOW
- **Arc**: Exploration → Frustrated Sign-Up → Superficial Browsing → Abandoned Commenting → Exit
  - **0.0s–7.51s**: Initial load; immediate intent to sign up (click "Sign Up").
  - **7.51s–53.53s**: Struggled with sign-up form (12 unknown clicks, 14 typing bursts). Abandoned after 46s.
  - **53.53s–56.4s**: Post-signup, returned to original post (API calls to ef376747).
  - **56.4s–103.76s**: Explored homepage (clicked "🗽 FunCity"), attempted to comment (68.29s–75.81s: 25 chars), but abandoned.
  - **103.76s–114.25s**: Viewed second post (6450bc39), clicked comments (110.29s), but no submission.
  - **114.25s–133.7s**: Returned to homepage, rapid scroll (115.39s), then exited via top-right clicks (132.26s, 133.7s).
- **Exit**: No clear trigger; final actions suggest frustration (rapid clicks in top-right, possibly closing tabs).
```

**Key Observations**:
- **High intent to engage** (sign-up + commenting) but **low follow-through** (abandoned 100% of comment attempts).
- **Desktop user with precise clicks** (e.g., (1536, 12) for "Sign Up") but **no deep content interaction**.
- **Technical issues likely**: Repeated API calls to the same endpoints (e.g., `/api/trending` 4x in 114s) may indicate loading errors or UI glitches.

You are a behavioral simulation model. Given a website description, generate a detailed behavioral profile describing how a user would interact with the website. Include: navigation pattern, reading behavior, engagement style, interaction speed, content preferences, typing behavior, feature discovery, and session flow with specific timings.

Website: https://fun-city-xi.vercel.app/
Description: FunCity is a Reddit-style NYC discovery board where users browse posts organized by NYC boroughs (The Bronx, Brooklyn, Manhattan, Queens, Staten Island) and topics (Art & Culture, Food & Eats, Hidden Gems, Nature & Parks, Nightlife). The homepage shows a feed of user posts sorted by Hot, New, or Top tabs. Each post card displays a borough tag, username, timestamp, title, body preview, upvote/downvote arrows with score, and comment count. The right sidebar contains borough filter buttons, topic filter buttons, and a Trending section showing top 5 posts. Users can click posts to see the full post detail page with a comments thread (each comment has upvote/downvote). There is a Sign Up button (top right) with a modal collecting username, password, age group, country, and NYC familiarity. Logged-in users see a "+ New Post" button and can comment and vote.

BEHAVIORAL PROFILE: WEB SESSION ANALYSIS

1. NAVIGATION PATTERN
- **Exploratory with focused bursts**: The user exhibits a non-linear, exploratory pattern with 3 distinct phases:
  *Phase 1 (0-32s)*: Rapid initial exploration (6 clicks in 13s, avg 2.2s between actions) with no clear target.
  *Phase 2 (35-65s)*: Focused engagement on a single post ("Met is okay") and signup flow (12 actions in 30s, avg 2.5s between actions).
  *Phase 3 (70-103s)*: Subreddit-hopping (3 subreddits in 30s) with content sampling.
- **Subreddit switching**: Visited 4 subreddits in 30s (brooklyn→queens→staten-island) via direct navigation (no search).
- **Backtracking**: Returned to "Sign Up" button twice (19.03s, 47.77s) with 28.74s gap, suggesting delayed intent.

2. READING BEHAVIOR
- **Skim-heavy with shallow dives**:
  - Initial scroll (24.26s): 566px in 0.6s (943px/s) – rapid scan.
  - Max scroll depth: 570px (≈2-3 post lengths on desktop).
  - Post engagement: Spent 1.5-3.5s on post pages before interacting (e.g., 35.48s click → 36.04s API call = 0.56s to trigger comment load).
  - No deep scrolls (>1000px) observed; all scrolls <62px after initial scan.
- **Comment reading**: Loaded comments for 3 posts (afb9fa2d, 8675b60b, cc5f7d0c) but only typed replies to 2 (e098fca7, cc5f7d0c), suggesting selective engagement.

3. ENGAGEMENT STYLE
- **Comment-first, vote-second**:
  - **Comments**: Typed replies to 2 posts (cc5f7d0c at 78.42s, e098fca7 at 92.76s) with 14s and 17s of typing respectively.
  - **Votes**: Only 1 vote recorded (86.42s, API: /api/votes) – vote-to-comment ratio = 0.5:1.
- **Latency**:
  - First comment attempt: 42.96s after session start (78.42s - 35.48s).
  - First vote: 51.17s after first post click (86.42s - 35.48s).
- **Abandoned actions**: Began typing in 3 instances but only submitted 2 comments (50.32s-52.8s typing not submitted).

4. INTERACTION SPEED
- **Fast but deliberate**:
  - **Early session**: 19.03s to first click (Sign Up), then 1.63s to next click (20.66s).
  - **Mid-session**: Avg 1.2s between clicks during signup flow (47.77s-65.77s).
  - **Late session**: Slowed to avg 2.8s between actions (82.44s-97.76s).
- **API triggers**: Clicks consistently triggered API calls within 0.03-0.15s (e.g., 35.48s click → 36.04s API).
- **Typing bursts**: 10-17 characters typed in 1.5-3.5s (avg 5-7 chars/s), with corrections (e.g., 96.31s-96.62s backspacing).

5. CONTENT PREFERENCES
- **Subreddit focus**: NYC-centric (4/4 subreddits visited):
  - brooklyn (39.72s)
  - queens (71.19s)
  - staten-island (83.92s)
  - Implied: "Met is okay" post (35.48s) likely in r/nyc or similar.
- **Post selection**: Clicked on 4 posts (afb9fa2d, 8675b60b, cc5f7d0c, e098fca7) with no clear topical pattern from IDs.
- **Trending reliance**: /api/trending called 8 times (0.02s, 36.07s, 37.87s, 39.75s, 46.59s, 71.23s, 74.11s, 83.94s) – likely used for navigation.

6. TYPING BEHAVIOR
- **Variable speed with corrections**:
  - **Short inputs**: 4-8 chars in 0.5-1.5s (e.g., 49.66s-50.32s: 4 chars in 0.66s).
  - **Long inputs**:
    - 20 chars in 1.0s (56.56s-56.57s, 20 chars/s).
    - 17 chars in 3.5s (92.76s-96.31s, 4.9 chars/s) with backspacing (96.31s-96.62s).
  - **Corrections**: 3 instances of backspacing (e.g., 96.31s: 17→16 chars).
- **Field ambiguity**: Typing occurred in unidentified fields (likely username/password during signup, then comment boxes).

7. FEATURE DISCOVERY
- **Used**:
  - Subreddit navigation (direct clicks on subreddit links).
  - Post voting (1 instance at 86.42s).
  - Commenting (2 submitted comments).
  - Signup flow (typed credentials at 49.66s-51.18s, submitted at 65.77s).
  - Trending/sort filters (API calls for /api/posts?sort=hot).
- **Ignored**:
  - Search functionality (no /api/search calls).
  - User profiles (no /api/users calls).
  - Post creation (no /api/posts calls beyond loading).
  - Advanced sorting (only "hot" used, no "new" or "top").

8. SESSION FLOW
- **Arc**: Exploration → Engagement → Browsing → Exit
  *0-20s*: Landing and initial scan
    - Loaded page, triggered 3 API calls (posts, trending, subreddits).
    - Rapid clicks on unknown elements (likely UI exploration).
  *20-36s*: Signup intent
    - Clicked "Sign Up" (19.03s), then abandoned.
    - Scrolled (24.26s) but no deep engagement.
  *36-66s*: Focused engagement
    - Clicked "Met is okay" post (35.48s), loaded comments (36.04s).
    - Returned to "Sign Up" (47.77s), completed signup (49.66s-65.77s).
  *66-87s*: Subreddit hopping
    - Navigated to brooklyn (39.72s), queens (71.19s), staten-island (83.92s).
    - Sampled 2 posts (cc5f7d0c, e098fca7), voted once (86.42s).
  *87-103s*: Commenting and exit
    - Typed and submitted 2 comments (78.42s-82.54s, 92.76s-97.92s).
    - No further actions after 98.06s (session ended at 103.63s).
- **Exit**: Abrupt termination after last comment API call (98.06s), no logout or navigation away recorded.

You are a behavioral simulation model. Given a website description, generate a detailed behavioral profile describing how a user would interact with the website. Include: navigation pattern, reading behavior, engagement style, interaction speed, content preferences, typing behavior, feature discovery, and session flow with specific timings.

Website: https://fun-city-xi.vercel.app/
Description: FunCity is a Reddit-style NYC discovery board where users browse posts organized by NYC boroughs (The Bronx, Brooklyn, Manhattan, Queens, Staten Island) and topics (Art & Culture, Food & Eats, Hidden Gems, Nature & Parks, Nightlife). The homepage shows a feed of user posts sorted by Hot, New, or Top tabs. Each post card displays a borough tag, username, timestamp, title, body preview, upvote/downvote arrows with score, and comment count. The right sidebar contains borough filter buttons, topic filter buttons, and a Trending section showing top 5 posts. Users can click posts to see the full post detail page with a comments thread (each comment has upvote/downvote). There is a Sign Up button (top right) with a modal collecting username, password, age group, country, and NYC familiarity. Logged-in users see a "+ New Post" button and can comment and vote.

You are a behavioral simulation model. Given a website description, generate a detailed behavioral profile describing how a user would interact with the website. Include: navigation pattern, reading behavior, engagement style, interaction speed, content preferences, typing behavior, feature discovery, and session flow with specific timings.

Website: https://fun-city-xi.vercel.app/
Description: FunCity is a Reddit-style NYC discovery board where users browse posts organized by NYC boroughs (The Bronx, Brooklyn, Manhattan, Queens, Staten Island) and topics (Art & Culture, Food & Eats, Hidden Gems, Nature & Parks, Nightlife). The homepage shows a feed of user posts sorted by Hot, New, or Top tabs. Each post card displays a borough tag, username, timestamp, title, body preview, upvote/downvote arrows with score, and comment count. The right sidebar contains borough filter buttons, topic filter buttons, and a Trending section showing top 5 posts. Users can click posts to see the full post detail page with a comments thread (each comment has upvote/downvote). There is a Sign Up button (top right) with a modal collecting username, password, age group, country, and NYC familiarity. Logged-in users see a "+ New Post" button and can comment and vote.

BEHAVIORAL PROFILE: WEB SESSION ANALYSIS

1. NAVIGATION PATTERN
- **Scattered with focused bursts**: The user exhibits a non-linear, exploratory pattern with rapid shifts between tasks. They jump between:
  - Sign-up flow (7.52s–32.53s)
  - Post/comment exploration (42.85s–58.27s)
  - Content creation (76.43s–106.56s)
- **No sustained browsing**: Only 1 scroll action (60.21s) over 12.66s, covering just 4px (0.38% of max depth), suggesting minimal vertical exploration.
- **High click density**: 28 clicks in 115s (~1 click every 4.1s), with 19 "unknown" clicks (68%) indicating either UI confusion or rapid feature testing.

2. READING BEHAVIOR
- **Skim-heavy**: The near-absent scroll depth (max 4px) and lack of hover/linger data suggest:
  - No deep reading of posts/comments (e.g., clicked post at 42.85s but no scroll to read content).
  - Visual scanning of surface-level elements (titles, thumbnails).
- **Time-to-click**: Average 3.2s between post load (42.85s) and first comment interaction (44.63s), implying rapid decision-making.

3. ENGAGEMENT STYLE
- **Comment-first, vote-later**: The user:
  - Typed 13+ character comments at 52.8s–56.69s (first comment) and 104.44s (second comment, 36 chars).
  - No explicit voting actions detected (no API calls to `/api/votes`).
- **Ratio**: 2 comments submitted (58.27s, 106.33s) with no recorded votes.
- **Speed**: First comment took 3.89s to type (52.8s–56.69s); second took 1.71s (104.44s–104.45s), suggesting pre-composed text or copy-paste.

4. INTERACTION SPEED
- **Fast but erratic**:
  - **Peak speed**: 0.09s between keystrokes during sign-up (e.g., 14.53s–15.41s).
  - **Slowdowns**: 12.66s scroll (60.21s) and 11.8s gap before second comment (96.15s–104.44s).
  - **Median action interval**: 1.8s (excluding typing bursts).
- **Sign-up urgency**: Completed 6-field form (username/password/email) in ~25s (7.52s–32.53s), with no visible corrections.

5. CONTENT PREFERENCES
- **Hot posts first**: Initial API calls fetch `/api/posts?sort=hot` (0.02s) and trending content (0.02s).
- **Targeted engagement**:
  - Clicked post at (304, 193) at 42.85s (likely a "hot" post).
  - Second post interaction at 106.19s (coordinates suggest a different post).
- **No subreddit focus**: `/api/subreddits` called 5 times but no clicks on subreddit links detected.

6. TYPING BEHAVIOR
- **High-speed input**:
  - **Sign-up**: 6-character username (14.53s–15.41s) and 6-character password (17.03s–17.96s) typed at ~0.18s/keystroke.
  - **Comments**:
    - First: 13 chars in 3.89s (~0.3s/keystroke).
    - Second: 36 chars in 1.71s (~0.05s/keystroke), likely copy-pasted.
- **No corrections**: No backspaces/deletions detected in any typing sequences.

7. FEATURE DISCOVERY
- **Used**:
  - Sign-up flow (7.52s–32.53s).
  - Commenting (52.8s–58.27s, 104.44s–106.33s).
  - Post navigation (42.85s, 106.19s).
  - Trending/hot feeds (API calls at 0.02s, 32.91s, 59.6s).
- **Ignored**:
  - Voting (no `/api/votes` calls).
  - Subreddit exploration (no clicks despite 5 API calls).
  - User profiles (no clicks on usernames/avatars).
  - Search (no `/api/search` calls).

8. SESSION FLOW
- **Phase 1: Immediate Sign-Up (0s–32.53s)**
  - Loaded page → clicked "Sign Up" at 7.52s → completed form in 25s (3 fields: username, password, email).
  - **Motivation**: Likely pre-planned (no hesitation before sign-up click).
- **Phase 2: Post-Sign-Up Exploration (32.91s–60.21s)**
  - Re-fetched hot posts (32.91s) → clicked first post at 42.85s → typed 13-character comment (52.8s–56.69s) → submitted at 58.27s.
  - **Behavior**: Rapid content sampling with minimal reading.
- **Phase 3: Browsing & Second Comment (60.21s–106.56s)**
  - Scrolled up slightly (60.21s) → clicked trending icon (74.55s) → typed 12-character search (76.43s–84.0s) → clicked second post (106.19s) → typed 36-character comment (104.44s–106.33s).
  - **Behavior**: Shifted to active participation (commenting) with no passive consumption.
- **Exit**: Session ended abruptly after second comment submission (no further actions post-106.56s).
```

**KEY INSIGHTS**:
- **Goal-oriented**: Prioritized sign-up and commenting over exploration.
- **Low friction tolerance**: No corrections in typing/form inputs; abandoned features quickly (e.g., subreddits).
- **Desktop efficiency**: Leveraged keyboard shortcuts/copy-paste (36-char comment in 1.71s).
- **No passive engagement**: Skipped voting/reading in favor of active participation.
